subject: DevOps
title: CircleCI and Travis CI
description: A micro-lesson that makes CircleCI and Travis CI usable.

hook.text: Before GitHub Actions, CircleCI and Travis CI were the darlings of open source CI/CD.

buildup.text: Travis CI pioneered free CI for open source. A .travis.yml file and a GitHub repo was all you needed.

discovery.text: CircleCI offered faster builds with better caching, parallelism, and a polished developer experience.

twist.text: GitHub Actions disrupted both. Free, integrated, and backed by Microsoft's resources. The market shifted overnight.

climax.text: CircleCI adapted and still thrives in enterprise. Travis CI struggled and was acquired. The market is brutal.

punchline.text: Pioneers don't always win. The CI/CD tool market moves fast.

quiz.question: What disrupted Travis CI and CircleCI's dominance?
options: GitHub Actions, which was free and natively integrated
A new programming language that didn't need CI
Cloud providers banning third-party CI tools
